key_insight: An analytic multi-occupancy trap model can predict hydrogen diffusion and retention in irradiated bcc metals with good agreement to atomistic simulations, without empirical fitting.
engineering_relevance: This provides a first-principles framework for forecasting hydrogen behavior in radiation-damaged tungsten and vanadium, which is important for fusion and nuclear materials design.
method: Molecular dynamics to compute effective hydrogen diffusivity in homogeneous monovacancy distributions and voids.
Lattice kinetic Monte Carlo to confirm rapid convergence of trap occupation to steady state.
Static/atomistic physics inputs from density functional theory or empirical potentials, with no fitted parameters.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports good agreement between full atomistic simulation and the new analytic diffusivity model across tungsten monovacancies, vanadium monovacancies, and tungsten voids.
watch_item: The approach relies on idealized homogeneous defect distributions and accurate input energetics, so transfer to complex, evolving irradiation microstructures may require additional validation.
paper_title: Multiscale modelling of diffusion and retention of hydrogen in multi-occupancy traps in irradiated bcc metals
